Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verb phrase; body-part noun object “कलजा” plus verbal noun/infinitive “खोवा”; commonly embedded with light verb “करैछे/होएछे”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To irritate, exhaust, or emotionally wear someone down, especially through stubborn or repeated behavior.
Usage Contexts: family scolding, childcare, daily complaints, informal storytelling
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in household speech of the Kishanganj-Seemanchal belt, where body-part metaphors express emotional strain.
First Example Sentence: ए बाच्चा त दिनभरि कलजा खोवा करैछे, एक बेरो चुप नाइ बसैछे।
Second Example Sentence: परीक्षा नजिक आछे, आ तोर ढिलाई मोर कलजा खोवा करैछे।
Third Example Sentence: बाजारमे ओकर झूठ-साँच सुनिके दुकानदारक कलजा खोवा होए गेल।

Idiom (Surjapuri): एक शीस कौनी गोटा घोरे दौनी
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Proverbial fixed clause; numeral phrase plus household-location phrase; elliptical predicate with rhythmic internal contrast.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A tiny matter is made into excessive noise, fuss, or public display while real work remains small.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: काम कम आ हल्ला बेसी करैछे।
Usage Contexts: village meetings, workplace criticism, politics, family humor
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Uses the imagery of a small grain causing movement across the whole house, reflecting rural domestic exaggeration humor.
First Example Sentence: काल्हिक बैठकमे रमजान एक शीस कौनी गोटा घोरे दौनी करलक, काम त एको नाइ बढ़लक।
Second Example Sentence: बियाहक तैयारीमे आवाज बेसी आछे; ए त एक शीस कौनी गोटा घोरे दौनी जइसन होए गेल।
Third Example Sentence: रिपोर्ट लिखैसँ पहिले टीम एक शीस कौनी गोटा घोरे दौनी नाइ करूक।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Condensed proverbial nominal clause; crop noun plus value noun; lacks overt finite verb, relying on parallel rural-market imagery.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: An early or standing opportunity can be valuable if recognized before others fully see its worth.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: मौका जल्दी बुझिके फायदा लेबाक चाही।
Usage Contexts: agriculture, bargaining, investment, advice, elder speech
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Connected to standing-crop trade and mustard/field valuation practices in agrarian Surjapuri-speaking communities.
First Example Sentence: अब्बू कहलै, कट्ठेय तोरी ढट्ठेय सोन, धान खड़ा रहिते दाम पक्का करि ल।
Second Example Sentence: किसान सभ हाटमे कट्ठेय तोरी ढट्ठेय सोन बुझिके अग्रिम सौदा करलक।
Third Example Sentence: जे मौका खेतमे आछे, ओकरा टाल नाइ; कट्ठेय तोरी ढट्ठेय सोन याद राख।

Idiom (Surjapuri): छुवा से बेसी छुवार गू भारी
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Comparative proverbial clause; noun phrase plus postposition “से” and comparative “बेसी”; earthy genitive construction “छुवार”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: The side burden, maintenance, or aftercare becomes harder than the main thing itself.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: मुख्य कामसँ बेसी ओकर झंझट भारी आछे।
Usage Contexts: family speech, finances, childcare, purchases, sarcastic complaints
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Draws from child-rearing labor and rural household realism; often used humorously despite its crude imagery.
First Example Sentence: छोट दुकान खोलली, मुदा लाइसेंस, भाड़ा आ हिसाबमे छुवा से बेसी छुवार गू भारी लागल।
Second Example Sentence: फोन सस्ता मिलल, मुदा मरम्मत आ रिचार्जमे छुवा से बेसी छुवार गू भारी होए गेल।
Third Example Sentence: बियाहक बरतन, तंबू, गीत—सभ मिलिके छुवा से बेसी छुवार गू भारी बनल।

Idiom (Surjapuri): नाती पुस्ले नतीजा भंगाँ फूटा भतीजा
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Kinship-based rhyming proverb; paired noun phrases with internal rhyme; elliptical contrast without explicit copula.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: One’s own close kin or trusted people usually matter differently from outsiders, especially in crisis.
Usage Contexts: family disputes, inheritance, crisis support, village counsel
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects extended-family social organization in the Surjapuri belt, where kinship categories guide duty, trust, and obligation.
First Example Sentence: संकट पड़ल त बुझायल, नाती पुस्ले नतीजा भंगाँ फूटा भतीजा, अपन घरवाला पहिले दौड़ल।
Second Example Sentence: पंचायतमे बूढ़ा कहलक, नाती पुस्ले नतीजा भंगाँ फूटा भतीजा, खूनक नाता जल्दी छूटै नाइ।
Third Example Sentence: मेला मे हराइ गेल बाच्चाक लेल पूरा परिवार जुटल, नाती पुस्ले नतीजा भंगाँ फूटा भतीजा।

Idiom (Surjapuri): बाप बेचै पान, बेटा शाहरुख खान
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Bipartite contrastive proverb; finite clause followed by nominal apposition; celebrity-name reference creates comic contrast.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone acts flashy, grand, or superior despite modest means or little achievement.
Usage Contexts: youth behavior, fashion, teasing, social media talk, village humor
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Modern Surjapuri humor blending local livelihood imagery with Bollywood celebrity culture.
First Example Sentence: नवा चश्मा लगाके ओइ एहन अकड़ैछे, जइसे बाप बेचै पान, बेटा शाहरुख खान।
Second Example Sentence: उधारक जूता पहिरिके तस्वीर खिंचाबैछे; गाँवक लोग कहैछे, बाप बेचै पान, बेटा शाहरुख खान।
Third Example Sentence: पढ़ाई छोड़ि केवल ठाठ देखाबै त बाप बेचै पान, बेटा शाहरुख खान कहाबै।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Adjectival-nominal idiom; adjective “लंबा” plus culturally marked noun “गरहोल”; used predicatively or as a vocative.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A very tall, lanky, or awkward-looking person, usually said jokingly rather than formally.
Usage Contexts: family teasing, village description, children’s humor, casual observation
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: A local descriptive expression relying on vivid body-shape comparison common in oral village humor.
First Example Sentence: छोट दरवाजामे शफीकक माथा लागल, दादी हँसिके कहलै, ए लंबा गरहोल धीरे चल।
Second Example Sentence: पुतुल नाचमे ओ लंबा गरहोल जइसन खड़ा रहल, सभ बाच्चा ताकैत रहल।
Third Example Sentence: खेतक आलि पर लंबा गरहोल सन छाया पड़ल त चरवाहा डरि गेल।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Elliptical contrastive proverb; locative phrase plus negator “नी”; second phrase intensifies desire/bragging through adjective “बड़का”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Having no resources while making large demands, plans, or claims.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: रुपैया नाइ आ मांग बहुत बड़का आछे।
Usage Contexts: money talk, wedding expenses, buying goods, political promises, family scolding
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Uses puffed-rice and pocket imagery from everyday rural food culture to mock empty showiness.
First Example Sentence: जेब खाली आ माग पाँच थान कपड़ा; ए त खोचात मूढ़ी नी, फोका बड़का।
Second Example Sentence: कमाई शुरू नाइ भेल, मुदा मोटरक सपना; खोचात मूढ़ी नी, फोका बड़का न कर।
Third Example Sentence: भोजक खर्च सुनिके काका कहलै, खोचात मूढ़ी नी, फोका बड़का, पहिले रुपैया जोड़।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Parallel locative nominal idiom; body-part locatives joined by contrast; no overt verb, meaning supplied pragmatically.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone speaks sweetly while hiding harmful intentions or betrayal.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: मीठ बोलैछे मुदा भीतर खराब इरादा राखैछे।
Usage Contexts: warnings, politics, friendship, bargaining, family disputes
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: A wider eastern Indo-Aryan style metaphor naturalized in Surjapuri speech through food and body imagery.
First Example Sentence: ओइ सामने मीठ बोलैछे, मुदा पीछे चाल चलैछे; मुँहमे गुड़, पेटमे छुरी।
Second Example Sentence: मेल-मिलापक बातमे मुँहमे गुड़, पेटमे छुरी वाला लोकसँ बचि रह।
Third Example Sentence: पंचायतमे सभे मुस्कुरायल, मुदा मोइ बुझली जे किछु लोकक मुँहमे गुड़, पेटमे छुरी आछे।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verbal idiom with numeral noun phrase and locative postposition; infinitival/light-verb construction used with “राखैछे/राखा”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To divide loyalty or effort between two incompatible choices, creating risk or indecision.
Usage Contexts: career choices, politics, relationships, business decisions, advice
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Riverine imagery suits the floodplain geography of the Surjapuri-speaking region, where boats are familiar everyday symbols.
First Example Sentence: तोइ दू नावमे पाँव राखा छोड़, एक काम पकड़ा आ ठीकसँ कर।
Second Example Sentence: चुनावमे ओइ दूनो दलक संग बोलैछे; गाँवक लोग कहैछे, दू नावमे पाँव राखैछे।
Third Example Sentence: पढ़ाई आ कारोबार दूनो चाही त समय बाँट, खाली दू नावमे पाँव राखा न चलै।
